title: Island Biogeography Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive p5.js simulation demonstrating how island size and distance from mainland affect species richness through immigration and extinction dynamics

learningObjectives: Predict how island size and distance from mainland affect species richness
Explain the equilibrium theory of island biogeography using immigration and extinction rates
Apply island biogeography principles to real-world conservation scenarios
prerequisites: Understanding of species diversity and richness
Basic knowledge of immigration and extinction

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive parameter exploration with real-time graphing
keyQuestions: Why does a larger island support more species at equilibrium?
How does distance from the mainland affect the rate of new species arriving?
What happens to the equilibrium point when you change only one variable?
How does this model apply to forest fragments surrounded by farmland?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think island size only matters because of physical space -- it also affects habitat diversity and population viability
Many students assume species accumulate indefinitely rather than reaching an equilibrium balance
Students may not realize that island biogeography applies to any isolated habitat, not just oceanic islands
assessmentOpportunities: Have students predict equilibrium species count for untested parameter combinations before running the simulation
Ask students to design a nature reserve system using island biogeography principles
Compare student data tables from the 2x2 exploration to identify which factor has a stronger effect
